segments.text: Hello. Welcome to my presentation,
navigating through the new Autodesk Platform Services business model,
and the best practices to manage your APS usage.
My name is Dwarnal Matha.
I'm a product manager working at Autodesk
on the Autodesk Platform Services Experience,
with a focus on the new APS business model and develop experiences.
Before I get started, I just want to quickly mention
our confidentiality statement,
making basically saying that any statements
I make in today's presentation are forward-looking statements
and are subject to changes moving forward.
Okay, so the Autodesk Platform Services offering.
At this point, I don't expect this to be new news,
because a lot of few customers have on boarded
to the new pricing model since the launch on December.
And the main reason we had a world-up pricing model
is to better support you as a customer,
as you scale using our APIs to support future innovation
in this agentic AI and MCP era.
And we wanted you to get the value and flexibility
our platform provides while using these APIs.
And that's one of the main reasons we revamped our offering.
So you as a customer might be someone being on a free tier,
you've already on boarded,
you've created the developer hub,
and you are using our APIs for free.
Or you're someone with a paid tier
who are using our APIs beyond a certain limit,
and you might be under the pre-pay model with tokens,
or you might on board the pay,
as you go model, which allows you a lot of usage
throughout the month,
and then you are built at the end of the month based on your usage.
So regardless of which customer you are,
you have on boarded to one of these three options.
So when we introduced this new business model at APS,
we thought that one thing which becomes very important
for you as a customer to resolve,
when you start on boarding to this business model,
is how do I better manage my APS subscription?
Because this was a very big change for us,
as well as you for a customer,
when you have on boarded to a business model
which did not exist before.
And we at APS wanted to make sure
you have all the necessary features,
the necessary support for you to on board to
and manage your APS subscription well.
And that is why you'll be seeing that
a lot of the features which we are releasing for you
as a customer, focus on two main things,
visibility into and control over your API usage.
And this is very important because when you are paying
for APIs or when you have on boarded to a new pricing model,
it becomes very important that you get visibility
and control over your usage.
So you can make better cost management decisions
within your business, within your development and more.
So let's first pick about control
and some of the features we already launched
for you to get supported,
to get better control over your APS usage.
The APS developer hub.
Firstly, I wanted the opportunity to thank you
as a customer for going through all the efforts
to migrate to the developer hub since December
because we made a mandatory for every customer
to use an APS developer hub.
And again, I've already mentioned this before,
but an APS developer hub is nothing
but a container which consists of your client IDs
and your entire engineering team.
So as an organization or all your client IDs
sitting in one place, making it very easy
for you to control the client IDs,
which are calling APS APIs
and also making it very easy to say,
go on your team, owns which applications
or who on your team are working on which applications.
Thus making it very easy for you to control APS usage.
And secondly, as a hub admin,
which we introduced as a new persona within the APS ecosystem,
we are really targeting features
for the hub admin slash tech admin slash developer admin persona
to make it really easy for the engineering manager
or the tech admin to better manage their APS costs
surrounding their development team.
So this is pretty much what a developer hub is.
And as you might already know,
you are already onboarded to this
because you made a mandatory since December.
With respect to the developer hub,
we introduced two new features in the last two weeks
focusing on making it easy for you as a customer
to use the developer hub.
And they're very simple features but very effective.
First one is bulk ad members.
We got several complaints from customers
that inviting one team member at a time
is taking too much of time
and that is not very scalable moving forward.
And that is why we introduced bulk ad members,
a very simple feature where you can invite
several people together.
And just making it very easy for your team
to onboard to the developer hub.
And secondly, we also introduced bulk app actions
because again, moving one app from one up to another
or deleting one app at a time is taking time
and it is not scalable.
And that is why bulk app actions allows you
to do app level management at scale,
making it very easy for you to do simple tasks
in the quickest amount of time.
Next up, which was a game changer
which we recently launched and that is
multiple developer hubs under one team.
When we launched the pricing model back in December,
we made a decision that one Autodesk team
can only have one developer hub.
And as you might already know, when I say Autodesk team,
I mean the team construct on manage.autodesk.com
or the accounts portal where you manage
your organizational subscriptions,
you manage your billing and more.
And with one APS subscription tied to one Autodesk team,
we were allowing only one developer hub.
And that turned out to be a big pain point
because different organizations are structured
different internally or globally.
And just having one developer hub is not scalable
because let's say within one organization,
there are two different teams across the organization
using APS, having client IDs and the same hub
mainly to security concerns,
mainly to privacy concerns and may also be confusing
as to because why would my team want to see
and other teams client IDs?
And that is why we now have the ability
to have up to five hubs every Autodesk team.
So if you are a company with a different
internal organizational structure,
if you have multiple locations across the globe
or if you have multiple cost centers at your company,
you can now have multiple hubs at under one team.
And you can have one specific hub for a specific use case,
for a specific location, for a specific cost center, et cetera.
Just making it very easy for you to use the APS subscription.
Nothing else changes.
All these five hubs still consume capacity
and use your APS subscription on the Autodesk team.
It's just more about you,
for you to better organize your applications
based on your use case.
So these are the current features
which we have for the control aspect.
And I will speak more about the upcoming features
in an upcoming slide.
Okay, moving on to visibility.
So visibility into APS usage is extremely important
and we have made sure we invested to providing you
a great experience when you are using our APIs.
And visibility into API usage is nothing
but your APS usage reporting features
across both portals, the accounts portal
and the developer portal.
So in the accounts portal,
if you are someone using flex tokens,
you're able to see your token balance.
You're able to see when your tokens are going to run out,
you will be able to see how many tokens you have consumed.
If you are someone with a free tier,
you're able to see the balance of the APIs,
you have included in that free tier,
making it very easy for you to manage your free subscription.
As a team admin, you're able to see the usage
for the entire team in one single place.
And we're also providing you the ability
to get granular hub level and app level reporting
in the accounts portal.
Now for the developer admin persona,
the developer admin persona is able to see the usage
of the applications inside their hub
or they're also able to see the hub level usage reporting
for their specific hub,
thus making sure they're able to track usage
for the entire team to make better decisions.
Now, as you can see,
one common theme across all these portals
is that we have made sure
that every potential persona is getting access to reporting.
So the team admin who's mainly making
the purchasing decisions are able to see
all the token amounts, the dollar amounts,
the currency amounts, the API usage for the entire team
on the team level, for them to make better decisions.
And they are also providing granularity
in that reporting to see the broken down usage
for a hub or an app for the team admin.
Then for the hub admin person,
the hub admin is also able to see the usage reporting
for their specific hub
and the developer is also able to see the usage reporting
for their specific application.
So as you can see,
we are really making sure every single persona
is getting some level of reporting
to make better decisions,
be it technical decisions,
if they're seeing any spikes,
being it purchasing decisions,
if they want to upgrade a tier and more.
So what are some best practices
while using our usage reporting features?
So if you're somewhere with a free tier,
get tracking which APIs your app is using
because you're just getting started with APS.
So you want to see what your baseline usage is
or should you potentially move to another tier
if you're consuming certain APIs
beyond a certain threshold?
If you are using a flex, a T flex,
when a pay as you go,
you're already building production level applications.
So you just want to see what the token amounts are,
what the currency amounts are.
You should identify high usage workflows
and monitor growth trends.
Because let's say if you are someone with a flex
and you're running out of flex tokens very quickly,
then pay as you go was an option for you
because then you can scale dynamically.
And then you, and at the end of the month,
you can just be charged based on all the usage you already did.
And then finally, if you're scaling across multiple
internal teams in our organization,
we recommend using pay as you go
because then you get unlimited usage
and then you get charged at the end of the month,
just making it very easy for you to scale
across your organization or two.
Okay, now that I've given some information
as to what we are already, what we've already launched,
let's speak more about what's coming.
So two months ago, we introduced that
and we announced that we will be introducing something
called as included usage.
So what is included usage?
So certain product subscriptions,
like let's say, form a build, for example,
will now have included API capacity with that subscription.
So more likely, you don't have to go and acquire
another APS offering.
Your current product subscription
will come with API capacity included with it.
And you can scan this QR code,
which will take you to the blog, which has more information.
But in general, these subscriptions
will just have some API capacity.
And for the may launch, we will be introducing
included capacity for the AC data model
and the manufacturing data model APIs,
which is again a new step towards our business model
because this is the first time we will be having
included API usage formally within a subscription,
also having the ability to track this
across both your portals too.
So now that we're introducing included usage,
I just wanna quickly give a highlight
as to what this will look like.
So let's say you have a team.
That team has five Mb of APS free
and almost 40 Mb of included
subscriber capacity across three different subscriptions.
You will see that all this capacity gets pulled together.
So what you're going to see is you're going to see
five plus 40, you'll see 45 Mb of API capacity
included within your Autodesk team.
And this is mainly to make sure that you get enough usage
above through the capacity you have
because let's say you have several subscriptions.
We just don't want you to get
included capacity for one subscription.
You should have the ability to use it across all subscriptions
and that's why it gets pulled together.
So it's a very simple way of understanding
as to how your capacity will work
and this will happen sometimes starting May 2026.
Okay, next up we are introducing a new feature
called notifications.
So notifications is at a point where it supports both.
It supports visibility
and it also supports control over your APS usage.
So as a team admin, you will be able to set up alerts
whenever your APIs are running out of capacity
or you're having a low balance for specific APIs.
So again, we're making it opt-in opt-out.
We're not making it completely opt-in
because if you don't want a notification,
you don't want a notification.
So we're giving you all the potential options
and these alerts will be for a specific API
and an engine under that API.
So let's say if you have automation,
then you have sub-five engines.
You can set it up at an engine level
at the most granular level.
And again, just making it very easy for the team admin
to get notified when they have a low balance
or when they are out of balance
because either in both those cases,
you might have to make a purchasing decision
because let's say if you've ran out of capacity,
you will have to refill tokens
or you'll have to check when is the next replenishment date
for your free tier or for your included with usage, et cetera.
So we are just making sure that you are getting notifications
whenever you run out of balances.
And with respect to this,
you will be able to select who gets notifications.
So again, the notifications will be via a banner
on the UI as well as emails.
Again, and we are making it opt-in opt-out
just to make sure you do not get any email fatigue
through this feature.
We are also launching a similar concept of notifications
for the developer hub,
mainly targeting the hub admin and the developer persona
because the hub admin will also want to see
which applications and their hub are going to be affected
if the team which owns that hub is running out of capacity
for a specific API or a sub API under that API.
And that is why we are introducing this feature
for the hub admin persona
to get low balance and zero balance notifications
whenever the team which owns that hub
is running out of capacity.
And again, this is also an opt-in opt-out feature
where you can opt-in or opt-out of having these notifications
sent to the hub admin or the app owner
depending on what fits you the best.
So this is a high level of notifications
which will be launched sometime in May
when we launch included usage too.
Okay.
And finally today's concept which I want to really
give a very quick overview on which you will hear
about moving forward is what is happening today
with respect to managing APS usage.
Today we have ISVs who are building applications
and they are paying for APS usage through their account
and their customers who are using the third party application
are paying the ISVs directly.
Or what is happening is that customers are sharing
their client ID to the ISV
and the ISV is using those credentials
to make API calls.
So yes, the customer is getting charged
but then there is a security concern
because sharing client ID is like this with partners
or with ISVs is not the most efficient
and most secure way of managing APS usage.
And looking at these two features
and these two current scenarios we realized
we have to build a feature which makes it very easy
for customers to use their API capacity
for a third party application.
And that is why we are going to be working on something
called as pass through usage.
Pass through usage is basically the ability for customers
to use their API capacity for a third party application.
So what does that mean?
Think of it that you have flex tokens
or you have included capacity with a former build subscription.
Your first option is, okay,
create a developer hub, create a client ID
and use a client ID to make API calls
and you're building your own application.
But you might also not want to build your own application.
You are already, you've downloaded an application
from the app store or your basically
just using a third party application.
So what do we do in that scenario?
And that's exactly what pass through is all about.
Because you've already, you already have all this API capacity
and these API balances and you want to consume that.
Now if you're not going to create your own application,
you want to consume that API capacity
through a third party application.
And that is exactly the definition of pass through.
It is the ability to use the API capacity
you own through a third party application.
Making it very easy for you as a customer
to use API capacity
and also making it very easy for an ISV
to manage their business model
and manage their apps, et cetera.
And this is something which we will be working on
in the next few months.
And we are also going to be starting a feature sponsorship program
which basically means that we are going to be working
with a close set of customers to see
and define this pass through feature
whenever we launch it in the next year or so.
And as you can see,
the main reason I wanted to speak to you about pass through
is that it has tremendous value
and we are really investing in visibility and control
because as you can see,
we are making it very easy for you as a customer
to consume your API capacity.
We're making it very easy for you as a customer
to leverage multiple opportunities
to consume API capacity
when it's going to be included
with certain of your product subscriptions
making you scale dynamically across the ABS platform.
And that's it from my side.
Thank you.
